paper_title: DocVQA: A Dataset for VQA on Document Images
evaluation_method: DocVQA is a visual question answering dataset with 50,000 questions on 12,767 document images (letters, memos, reports etc.). The evaluation metric is Average Normalized Levenshtein Similarity (ANLS). For each question-answer pair, the normalized Levenshtein distance NL(prediction, gold) is computed. If NL < 0.5 (i.e., edit distance is less than half the max string length), the score is 1 - NL; otherwise the score is 0. This threshold distinguishes correctly selected but poorly OCR-recognized answers from entirely wrong answers. The ANLS is the average of these scores across all questions. ANLS is case-insensitive but space-sensitive.
